ECC CSR Generator & Compatibility Checker

This tool prepares CSR guidance for SSL/TLS using ECC P-384 and checks known compatibility issues found during previous implementation cases.

1. Certificate Request Details
FQDN of the server or website.
Separate multiple SAN values with commas.
2. Platform & Web Stack
Examples: Windows Server 2012, CentOS 7, Rocky Linux 8.
Linux guidance For Linux, this tool generates an OpenSSL command using ECC P-384 with SHA-256. Private key stays on the host under agency control.
3. Generate
4. Output
5. Minimum Baseline Reference
Component Minimum Baseline Reason for Concern
OpenSSL 1.1.1 and above Older cryptographic libraries may fail or behave inconsistently with ECC CSR generation.
OpenSSH 1.1.1 and above Used here as part of the current knowledge bank rule set supplied for your environment.
Windows Server 2012 and above Older Windows crypto stack may not support ECC workflows cleanly.
Linux OS CentOS 7 and above Older builds often come with outdated libraries or unsupported defaults.
XAMPP 7.4.33 and above Bundled components may be too old for stable ECC handling.
Apache 2.4.54 and above Older server builds may cause TLS or ECC handling issues.
PHP 7.4.33 and above Older versions may indicate an outdated overall stack.
CSR handling Original file only Do not copy-paste CSR through UI, email, PDF, or WhatsApp. Formatting damage can corrupt the request.
ECC target: 384-bit EC curve (secp384r1 / P-384). Hash: SHA-256. This tool is intended as a preventive check before SSL/TLS application submission.
